name: cua-toolkit
description: Autonomous desktop computer-use for AGNT, powered by the Cua Driver (trycua/cua). Lets agents see windows, read accessibility trees + screenshots, click elements by index, type text, and run a full autonomous goal loop - all in the background without stealing your cursor or focus. Self-contained: cua-setup action=ensure installs the driver + starts the daemon in one call, and every tool auto-starts the daemon on demand.
author: Annie (AGNT)

schema.title: Cua Setup & Doctor
schema.description: Install, verify, and manage the Cua Driver. ensure=ONE-CALL bootstrap (install if missing + start daemon + doctor) - run this first and you are done. Other actions: status, doctor (Session-0 check), install, serve, stop, version.

action.description: ensure=ONE-CALL bootstrap (install if missing + start daemon + doctor health/Session-0 probe); status=check install+daemon; doctor=full health; install=installer only; serve=start daemon; stop=stop daemon; version=print driver version.

confirm.description: Required true for ensure/install/serve/stop (may install software / change state).

success.description: True when the requested action completed (and, for ensure, the session is interactive).

installed.description: Whether the cua-driver binary is currently installed and resolvable.

version.description: Installed cua-driver version (null when not installed).

binaryPath.description: Resolved path to the cua-driver binary (status action).

daemon.description: Daemon state for status action: { running: boolean }.

daemonRunning.description: Whether the cua-driver daemon is running (ensure action).

daemonStarted.description: Whether the serve action successfully started the daemon.

stopped.description: Whether the stop action successfully stopped the daemon.

session0Warning.description: True when the driver is in Windows Session 0 (no interactive desktop) — window tools will return empty.

session0Hint.description: Remediation guidance when session0Warning is true.

report.description: Full driver doctor/health report (doctor and ensure actions).

installerOutput.description: Tail of the installer output (install action).

steps.description: Ordered bootstrap steps the tool executed (ensure action).

error.description: Error message when success=false (null on success).

schema.title: Cua List Windows
schema.description: Read-only. Enumerate all open desktop windows the driver can see, returning each window's pid, windowId, title, and app. Safe - no clicking. Auto-starts the daemon if it is down. Use a window's pid/windowId with cua-observe or cua-act.

filter.description: Optional case-insensitive substring to filter windows by title or app name (e.g. 'chrome', 'notepad').

count.description: Number of windows returned (after the optional filter).

windows.description: Array of window descriptors: { pid, windowId, title, app, raw }. Use pid+windowId with cua-observe / cua-input / cua-act.

warning.description: Set when the result is empty — usually indicates Windows Session 0 (non-interactive driver).

hint.description: Suggested next step (e.g. call cua-observe with a chosen pid/windowId).

bootstrap.description: Ordered self-heal steps the tool ran to bring the daemon up (empty when nothing was needed).

schema.title: Cua Observe Window
schema.description: Read-only. Capture a window's state: accessibility tree (every interactive element with its index) plus an optional screenshot rendered inline. This is the 'see the screen' call that feeds the autonomous loop. No clicks performed. Auto-starts the daemon if down.

captureMode.description: som=tree+screenshot (default, best for reasoning); ax=tree only (cheap, no capture); vision=screenshot only (pixel-grounded).

showImage.description: If true and a screenshot was captured, render it inline in chat.

success.description: True when the window state was captured.

windowId.description: The window id that was observed.

captureMode.description: Capture mode actually used: som | ax | vision.

elementCount.description: Number of interactive elements detected in the accessibility tree.

elements.description: Structured element list: each entry is { index, role, name, value }. Pass `index` to cua-input action=click.

treeMarkdown.description: Human-readable accessibility tree, with [element_index N] markers — good for LLM reasoning.

hasScreenshot.description: Whether a screenshot was captured (false for ax-only mode).

imageHtml.description: Inline tag with the screenshot embedded as a data URL (null when no screenshot).

hint.description: Suggested next step (e.g. how to act on an element).

bootstrap.description: Ordered self-heal steps the tool ran to bring the daemon up (empty when nothing was needed).

rawKeys.description: Keys present in the underlying driver response (debugging aid).

schema.title: Cua Input (Click / Type / Hotkey)
schema.description: Low-level primitive input on a real window. action=click clicks an element by its index (from cua-observe); type types text; hotkey presses a key combo; launch_app opens an app. Background dispatch - does NOT steal cursor/focus. Requires confirm=true.

action.description: click=click element index; type=type_text; hotkey=key combo; launch_app=open app by name/id.

elementIndex.description: Element index to click (action=click). Get it from cua-observe.

text.description: Text to type (action=type), key combo like 'ctrl+s' (action=hotkey), or app name/bundle id (action=launch_app).

confirm.description: Safety gate. Must be true to actually dispatch input to your desktop.

success.description: True when the input action was dispatched without driver error.

dispatched.description: Whether real input was actually sent (false when blocked by confirm=false or validation).

action.description: Echoed input action: click | type | hotkey | launch_app.

elementIndex.description: Element index that was clicked (action=click only).

text.description: Echoed text payload — typed text, hotkey combo, or launched app name (action-dependent).

result.description: Parsed driver response for the dispatched input.

hint.description: Suggested follow-up (typically re-observe to verify the effect).

error.description: Error message when success=false (null on success).

schema.title: Cua Autonomous Act
schema.description: High-level autonomous loop. Give a natural-language goal and a target window; the tool observes (tree+screenshot) and returns the current state plus a structured plan for the calling agent to drive the observe/reason/act/verify loop. maxSteps caps actions. dryRun returns the plan without executing. Requires confirm=true to execute real input.

goal.description: Natural-language objective, e.g. 'open the File menu and click Save As'.

dryRun.description: If true, return the observed state + planned first action WITHOUT executing (safe planning mode).

confirm.description: Safety gate. Must be true (and dryRun=false) to actually drive the desktop.

success.description: True when the window state was observed and a plan was produced.

windowId.description: The window id that was observed/targeted.

dryRun.description: Whether this call was a dry run (planning only, no input dispatched).

canExecute.description: True when dryRun=false AND confirm=true — cua-input may now drive the desktop.

elementCount.description: Number of interactive elements detected in the accessibility tree.

elements.description: Structured element list: { index, role, name, value }.

treeMarkdown.description: Human-readable accessibility tree with [element_index N] markers.

hasScreenshot.description: Whether a screenshot was captured for this observation.

imageHtml.description: Inline tag with the screenshot embedded as a data URL.

plan.description: Structured plan envelope: { goal, target, maxSteps, mode, elementsAvailable, instructionsForAgent }. Drives the observe/reason/act/verify loop.

note.description: Human-readable status note describing the current mode (dry-run / live / blocked).

bootstrap.description: Ordered self-heal steps the tool ran to bring the daemon up (empty when nothing was needed).
